name: PHI / PII redactor
task: Redact protected health information and personally identifiable information from free-text clinical and customer-support notes. Replace names, dates, MRN/SSN, addresses, phone numbers, and emails with role-tagged placeholders (e.g. [PATIENT], [DOB], [MRN], [ADDRESS]). Preserve clinical content verbatim.
verifier: On a 250-row holdout of synthetic-but-realistic PHI: recall on PII spans >= 0.98 (false-negative on a real identifier is the failure mode), precision >= 0.95 (don't over-redact clinical terms), exact-tag match >= 0.90, zero hallucinated content outside the redaction tags.
notes: Higher accuracy weight (0.50) and higher gate (0.90) than general models: a missed identifier in production is a HIPAA incident, so we accept smaller models on size/cost trade-offs to push the recall ceiling. Recall is asymmetrically more important than precision — over-redacting a clinical term is a usability bug, under-redacting an identifier is a breach. Pair with a rule-based pre-pass for SSN/phone/email regex catches before model inference. Production deployments should add domain-specific seed data (cardiology, oncology, etc.) — 200 generic seeds here is a smoke-test starter; ship-grade redactors use 2-5k pairs.
